distractors.label: A high-priority instruction supplied by the AI system to steer behavior during the current run.
The limited amount of tokens or media the model can consider at once.
The input given to the model, including user text, system instructions, conversation history, retrieved material, or other context.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. Input context is everything currently visible to the model: prompt tokens, conversation history, retrieved documents, and already-generated response tokens.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for System prompt. Input context is everything currently visible to the model: prompt tokens, conversation history, retrieved documents, and already-generated response tokens.

distractors.label: The rules and vocabulary that split text into tokens and map those tokens to IDs.
A model-readable chunk of text, such as a word, word piece, punctuation mark, space, or other tokenizer chunk.
The number assigned to a token so the model can look up its learned starting vector.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. Tokenization is the step that splits text into model-readable tokens that can be represented as token IDs.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for Tokenizer. Tokenization is the step that splits text into model-readable tokens that can be represented as token IDs.

distractors.label: A durable store of information available later.
A learned starting vector retrieved for a token ID.
A learned number inside the model that shapes how signals are transformed.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. Hidden state is a temporary context-shaped internal vector for a token at a particular model layer.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for Memory. Hidden state is a temporary context-shaped internal vector for a token at a particular model layer.

distractors.label: Connecting a model response to available evidence, such as retrieved documents, cited sources, data, or tool results.
Finding external material and placing it into the current context for the model to use.
Additional targeted training after broad pretraining.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. RAG is retrieval-augmented generation: retrieving outside information and placing it into the model context before generation.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for Grounding. RAG is retrieval-augmented generation: retrieving outside information and placing it into the model context before generation.

distractors.label: A model-assigned chance for a candidate next token, not a truth guarantee.
The decoding step that chooses one generated token from next-token probabilities.
Raw scores for possible next tokens before probabilities.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. Softmax is a function that turns next-token raw scores into probabilities that sum to one.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for Probability. Softmax is a function that turns next-token raw scores into probabilities that sum to one.

distractors.label: The policies, processes, oversight, evaluation, and accountability structures that guide AI use.
The electricity required to train, run, cool, and support an AI system.
A facility that houses servers, networking, power, and cooling for digital services, including AI workloads.

cases.correctFeedback: Insight strengthened. Water use is water that may be used directly or indirectly for cooling and electricity generation around AI infrastructure.
cases.wrongFeedback: Not quite. That description is for Governance. Water use is water that may be used directly or indirectly for cooling and electricity generation around AI infrastructure.
